sourceMovie.title: Ted 2
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Ted 2 (2015) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ted
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Ted (2012) is the kind of film Ted 2 fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The comedy overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Mask
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Mask shares Ted 2's gift for comedy — the 1994 film delivers fantasy without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Abominable
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Ted 2 compelling — the comedy, the fantasy — shows up in Abominable (2019) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Herbie Rides Again
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The comedy energy of Ted 2 is alive in Herbie Rides Again (1974), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Moana
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Moana (2016) operates in the same comedy as Ted 2, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: 22 Jump Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: 22 Jump Street (2014) belongs to the same conversation as Ted 2 — both anchor their comedy in crime emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ted 2's comedy stayed with you, Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa (2008) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The adventure through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Pinocchio
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Ted 2 excels in fantasy, Pinocchio (1940) doubles down on animation to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: American Reunion
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: American Reunion (2012) is the natural next step after Ted 2: same comedy DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and craft elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Bad Boys: Ride or Die
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Ted 2 who respond to its comedy will find Bad Boys: Ride or Die (2024) immediately familiar. Both films share a action sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ted 2?
faq.answer: Try Ted, The Mask, Abominable—each shares DNA with Ted 2 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ted 2?
faq.answer: Ted 2 (2015) is best described as Comedy, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ted 2 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ted Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
